packages.name: migrate
packages.synopsis: Package migrate is a small, dependency-free, ActiveRecord-style schema migration toolkit for Go built entirely on top of the standard library's database/sql package.
packages.doc:
Package migrate is a small, dependency-free, ActiveRecord-style schema
migration toolkit for Go built entirely on top of the standard library's
database/sql package.

# Overview

A [Migration] carries a monotonically increasing Version, a human readable
Name, and a pair of directions. A direction may be expressed either as a Go
function that runs against an open *sql.Tx, or as raw SQL text. A [Migrator]
wraps a *sql.DB, ensures a bookkeeping table (schema_migrations by default)
exists, records which versions have been applied, and drives the migrations
forward and backward.

Every migration runs inside its own transaction. If a migration fails, its
transaction is rolled back, the version is not recorded, and the run halts
immediately without touching any later migration. Re-running [Migrator.Migrate]
is therefore idempotent: only versions that have not yet been recorded are
applied.

# Loading migrations

Migrations can be registered programmatically with [Migrator.Register] (handy
for Go migrations) or loaded from a directory / io/fs.FS of files named

	<version>_<name>.up.sql
	<version>_<name>.down.sql

via [LoadDir] and [LoadFS]. See those functions for the exact grammar.

# Schema DSL

The package ships an ActiveRecord-flavoured schema builder. Package-level
helpers ([CreateTable], [AddColumn], [AddIndex], [ChangeTable],
[AddForeignKey], [AddReference], [RenameColumn], [ChangeColumn], ...) emit SQL
strings you can drop straight into a migration's UpSQL/DownSQL or execute from
a Go migration. The full column vocabulary is available on [Table]: String,
Text, Integer, BigInteger, Float, Decimal, Boolean, Date, Time, Timestamp
(with [Precision] and [WithTimezone]), Binary, JSON, JSONB, UUID, Enum, and
[Array] columns, plus References.

# Dialects

A [Dialect] captures how a backend quotes identifiers, spells bind
parameters, declares auto-increment primary keys, and names concrete column
types. Four dialects are provided: [ANSI] (the default), [Postgres], [MySQL],
and [SQLite]. The package-level helpers render for [ANSI]; bind another
dialect with [NewSchema] to get, for example, backtick quoting and
AUTO_INCREMENT for MySQL, or bigserial and native jsonb/array/uuid types for
PostgreSQL.

# Reversible changes

[Change] records a sequence of schema operations and derives the inverse
automatically, so a migration is written once and rolled back for free.
Operations that cannot be mechanically reversed (a raw Execute, a change of
column type, or a drop without a rebuild block) mark the change irreversible
and rolling it back fails with [ErrIrreversibleMigration].

# Seeds and dumps

[Seeder] loads data idempotently, tracking each named seed so it runs at most
once. [SchemaDump] collects DDL into a single version-stamped, reconstructable
script analogous to Rails' schema.rb.

# SQL dialect assumptions

The migrator's own bookkeeping statements target a conservative ANSI SQL
subset regardless of the schema dialect chosen:

  - Bookkeeping bind parameters use the '?' placeholder style (as used by
    MySQL, SQLite, and the in-memory test driver). Use [Dialect.Placeholder]
    to render backend-appropriate markers in your own statements.
  - The bookkeeping auto-increment key is emitted as
    "BIGINT GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY" (SQL:2003 identity columns).
  - Referential columns created by [Table.References] follow the Rails
    convention of "<singular>_id" and a naive "+s" pluralisation for the
    referenced table name.

These choices are documented so callers know exactly what to expect; they are
deliberately not clever, because the goal is predictable, greppable output.

consts.doc:
DefaultSeedTable is the bookkeeping table used to track applied seeds when no
other name is configured.

DefaultTable is the name of the bookkeeping table used when no other name is
configured via [WithTable].
Version is the semantic version of this library. It mirrors the VERSION file.

vars.doc: Sentinel errors returned by the migrator, loaders and schema DSL.

types.name: AlterTable
types.doc:
AlterTable accumulates alterations inside a [Schema.ChangeTable] block and
renders them as a sequence of ALTER TABLE / CREATE INDEX statements. It
mirrors ActiveRecord's change_table bulk-alter builder: each call appends one
operation, and the whole block is emitted as ";\n"-joined statements in call
order.

methods.name: BigInteger
methods.doc: BigInteger adds a BIGINT column.

methods.name: Boolean
methods.doc: Boolean adds a BOOLEAN column.

methods.name: Change
methods.doc: Change alters the type of an existing column.

methods.name: Column
methods.doc: Column adds a column with an explicit SQL type spelling.

methods.name: Index
methods.doc: Index adds an index over columns (see [Schema.AddIndex]).

methods.name: Integer
methods.doc: Integer adds an INTEGER column.

methods.name: References
methods.doc: References adds a reference column (see [Schema.AddReference]).

methods.name: Remove
methods.doc: Remove drops one or more columns.

methods.name: RemoveIndex
methods.doc: RemoveIndex drops a named index.

methods.name: Rename
methods.doc: Rename renames a column.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String adds a VARCHAR column.

methods.name: Text
methods.doc: Text adds a TEXT column.

methods.name: Timestamp
methods.doc: Timestamp adds a TIMESTAMP column.

methods.name: Timestamps
methods.doc: Timestamps adds created_at and updated_at NOT NULL timestamp columns.

types.name: ChangeRecorder
types.doc:
ChangeRecorder captures a sequence of schema operations so a single "change"
definition can be run forward and, when every operation is reversible,
automatically reversed. It mirrors ActiveRecord's reversible migrations: you
describe the change once, and the inverse is derived from it.

Operations that cannot be mechanically inverted (a raw [ChangeRecorder.Execute],
a [ChangeRecorder.ChangeColumn], or a [ChangeRecorder.DropTable] without a
rebuild block) mark the change irreversible; attempting to roll such a change
back fails with [ErrIrreversibleMigration].

methods.name: AddCheckConstraint
methods.doc:
AddCheckConstraint records adding a CHECK constraint; its inverse removes it.
The same table and expression reproduce the constraint name on rollback.

methods.name: AddColumn
methods.signature: func (r *ChangeRecorder) AddColumn(table, name, sqlType string, opts ...ColumnOption)
methods.doc: AddColumn records adding a column; its inverse is DROP COLUMN.

methods.name: AddForeignKey
methods.doc: AddForeignKey records adding a foreign key; its inverse removes it.

methods.name: AddIndex
methods.doc: AddIndex records adding an index; its inverse drops it by name.

methods.name: AddReference
methods.doc: AddReference records adding a reference column; its inverse removes it.

methods.name: AddTimestamps
methods.doc: AddTimestamps records adding created_at/updated_at; its inverse removes them.

methods.name: AddUniqueConstraint
methods.doc: AddUniqueConstraint records adding a UNIQUE constraint; its inverse removes it.

methods.name: ChangeColumn
methods.signature: func (r *ChangeRecorder) ChangeColumn(table, name, sqlType string, opts ...ColumnOption)
methods.doc:
ChangeColumn records a column type change. Because the prior type is not
captured, the change is irreversible.

methods.name: CreateJoinTable
methods.signature: func (r *ChangeRecorder) CreateJoinTable(table1, table2 string, build func(t *Table), opts ...TableOption)
methods.doc:
CreateJoinTable records creating a many-to-many join table; its inverse drops
it.

methods.name: CreateTable
methods.signature: func (r *ChangeRecorder) CreateTable(name string, build func(t *Table), opts ...TableOption)
methods.doc: CreateTable records a table creation; its inverse is DROP TABLE.

methods.name: CreateView
methods.doc:
CreateView records creating a view; its inverse drops it. When the view is
[Materialized], the drop targets a materialized view too.

methods.name: Dialect
methods.doc: Dialect returns the dialect the recorder renders statements for.

methods.name: DropTable
methods.signature: func (r *ChangeRecorder) DropTable(name string, build func(t *Table), opts ...TableOption)
methods.doc:
DropTable records a table drop. It is reversible only when build is non-nil
(describing how to recreate the table); otherwise the change is irreversible.

methods.name: EnableExtension
methods.doc:
EnableExtension records enabling a PostgreSQL extension; its inverse disables
it.

methods.name: Execute
methods.doc:
Execute records a raw SQL statement. Raw SQL cannot be inverted, so the change
is irreversible.

methods.name: RemoveColumn
methods.signature: func (r *ChangeRecorder) RemoveColumn(table, name, sqlType string, opts ...ColumnOption)
methods.doc:
RemoveColumn records dropping a column. It is reversible only when sqlType is
given (so the column can be re-added); otherwise the change is irreversible.

methods.name: RenameColumn
methods.doc: RenameColumn records a column rename; its inverse renames back.

methods.name: RenameIndex
methods.doc: RenameIndex records renaming an index; its inverse renames back.

methods.name: RenameTable
methods.doc: RenameTable records a table rename; its inverse renames back.

methods.name: Reversible
methods.doc: Reversible reports whether every recorded operation can be inverted.

types.name: ColumnOption
types.doc: ColumnOption modifies a column definition.

funcs.name: Array
funcs.doc:
Array marks the column as an array of its base type. Native arrays are only
emitted for the PostgreSQL and ANSI dialects; MySQL and SQLite fall back to a
JSON column.

funcs.name: Default
funcs.doc:
Default sets a column default. String values are emitted as quoted SQL
literals; all other values use their default Go formatting (numbers, bools).
Use [DefaultRaw] for expressions such as CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.

funcs.name: DefaultRaw
funcs.doc: DefaultRaw sets a column default to a raw SQL expression, emitted verbatim.

funcs.name: Limit
funcs.doc: Limit sets the length for VARCHAR columns (ignored by other types).

funcs.name: NotNull
funcs.doc: NotNull marks the column NOT NULL. Columns are nullable by default.

funcs.name: Precision
funcs.doc:
Precision sets the total number of digits for DECIMAL columns or the
fractional-second precision for TIMESTAMP/TIME columns.

funcs.name: PrimaryKey
funcs.doc: PrimaryKey marks the column as the table's PRIMARY KEY.

funcs.name: Scale
funcs.doc:
Scale sets the number of digits to the right of the decimal point for DECIMAL
columns.

funcs.name: Unique
funcs.doc: Unique adds a UNIQUE constraint to the column.

funcs.name: WithTimezone
funcs.doc:
WithTimezone renders a TIMESTAMP/TIME column as WITH TIME ZONE where the
dialect supports it.

types.name: ConstraintOption
types.doc:
ConstraintOption modifies a table constraint statement produced by
[Schema.AddCheckConstraint], [Schema.AddUniqueConstraint], and their removers.

funcs.name: ConstraintName
funcs.doc:
ConstraintName overrides the generated constraint name. When omitted, a
deterministic name is derived from the table and the constraint body so that
the matching "remove" helper reproduces the same name.

funcs.name: Deferrable
funcs.doc:
Deferrable renders the constraint as DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED. It applies
to UNIQUE constraints (PostgreSQL); dialects and constraint kinds that do not
support deferral ignore it.

types.name: Dialect
types.doc:
Dialect abstracts the SQL differences between database backends: how
identifiers are quoted, how bind parameters are spelled, how the automatic
primary-key column is declared, and how the DSL's abstract column types map
to concrete SQL. The package ships four implementations, exposed as the
package-level values [ANSI], [Postgres], [MySQL], and [SQLite].

Dialect is a sealed interface: it has unexported methods, so only the
dialects provided by this package satisfy it. Select one by name with
[DialectByName] and bind it to a schema builder with [NewSchema].

vars.doc: The four dialects supported by the package.

funcs.name: DialectByName
funcs.doc:
DialectByName returns the [Dialect] registered under name (case-insensitive),
or [ErrUnknownDialect] if none matches. Accepted names are "ansi",
"postgres" (or "postgresql"/"pg"), "mysql" (or "mariadb"), and "sqlite" (or
"sqlite3").

types.name: EnumValueOption
types.doc: EnumValueOption positions a new value within an existing enumerated type.

funcs.name: AfterValue
funcs.doc: AfterValue inserts the new enum value immediately after an existing value.

funcs.name: BeforeValue
funcs.doc: BeforeValue inserts the new enum value immediately before an existing value.

types.name: ForeignKeyOption
types.doc: ForeignKeyOption modifies an [Schema.AddForeignKey] statement.

funcs.name: FKColumn
funcs.doc: FKColumn overrides the referencing column (default "<singular-to>_id").

funcs.name: FKName
funcs.doc: FKName overrides the generated constraint name.

funcs.name: FKPrimaryKey
funcs.doc:
FKPrimaryKey overrides the referenced column on the target table (default
"id").

funcs.name: OnDelete
funcs.doc: OnDelete sets the ON DELETE referential action.

funcs.name: OnUpdate
funcs.doc: OnUpdate sets the ON UPDATE referential action.

types.name: IndexOption
types.doc: IndexOption modifies a CREATE INDEX statement.

funcs.name: IndexName
funcs.doc: IndexName overrides the generated index name.

funcs.name: UniqueIndex
funcs.doc: UniqueIndex makes the index UNIQUE.

funcs.name: Using
funcs.doc:
Using selects the index method (e.g. "gin", "gist", "hash", "btree"),
emitted as "USING <method>".

funcs.name: Where
funcs.doc:
Where adds a WHERE clause, producing a partial index. The condition is
emitted verbatim.

types.name: MigrateFunc
types.signature: type MigrateFunc func(ctx context.Context, tx *sql.Tx) error
types.doc:
MigrateFunc is the signature of a Go migration direction. It receives the
per-migration transaction and must not commit or roll it back; the [Migrator]
owns the transaction lifecycle.

types.name: Migration
types.doc:
Migration describes a single, reversible change to the database schema.

A migration is identified by its Version, which must be unique within a
[Migrator] and is used to order migrations. The direction to apply may be
given either as a Go function (Up / Down) or as SQL text (UpSQL / DownSQL).
When a Go function is present it takes precedence over the SQL text for that
direction.

SQL text may contain multiple statements separated by semicolons; each
non-empty statement is executed in order within the migration's transaction.

funcs.name: Change
funcs.signature: func Change(version uint64, name string, fn func(r *ChangeRecorder)) Migration
funcs.doc:
Change builds a reversible [Migration] from a change definition, using the
[ANSI] dialect. See [ChangeWith] for the dialect-aware form.

funcs.name: ChangeWith
funcs.signature: func ChangeWith(d Dialect, version uint64, name string, fn func(r *ChangeRecorder)) Migration
funcs.doc:
ChangeWith builds a reversible [Migration] from a change definition rendered
for dialect d. The migration's Up runs the recorded operations forward; its
Down runs the automatically derived inverse in reverse order. If any recorded
operation is irreversible, rolling the migration back fails with
[ErrIrreversibleMigration].

funcs.name: LoadDir
funcs.doc:
LoadDir loads migrations from files in the given directory. It is a thin
wrapper around [LoadFS] using os.DirFS.

funcs.name: LoadFS
funcs.doc:
LoadFS loads migrations from the root of fsys. Files must be named
"<version>_<name>.up.sql" and "<version>_<name>.down.sql". The up file is
required; the down file is optional (its absence yields an irreversible
migration). Files that do not match the naming pattern are ignored.

The returned slice is sorted ascending by version. An error is returned for
duplicate versions, unreadable files, or an up/down pair whose names disagree.

types.name: MigrationStatus
types.doc: MigrationStatus describes whether a known migration has been applied.

types.name: Migrator
types.doc:
Migrator applies and reverses [Migration]s against a *sql.DB, tracking which
versions have been applied in a bookkeeping table.

A Migrator is safe to construct with [New] and configure with [Option]s. Its
methods are not safe for concurrent use from multiple goroutines against the
same database; run migrations from a single goroutine.

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc:
New creates a Migrator over db. It panics only on a programming error such as
a nil db; configuration problems (like an invalid table name) surface from
the first method call. Use [Migrator.Validate] to check eagerly.

methods.name: Down
methods.signature: func (mg *Migrator) Down(ctx context.Context, n int) error
methods.doc:
Down rolls back the n most recently applied migrations in descending order.
If n <= 0 every applied migration is rolled back.

methods.name: EnsureSchemaTable
methods.doc:
EnsureSchemaTable creates the bookkeeping table if it does not already exist.
It is called implicitly by the other methods but is exported for callers that
want to provision the table up front.

methods.name: Migrate
methods.doc:
Migrate applies every pending migration in ascending order. It is idempotent:
migrations already recorded are skipped. A failing migration halts the run.

methods.name: MigrateTo
methods.signature: func (mg *Migrator) MigrateTo(ctx context.Context, target uint64) error
methods.doc:
MigrateTo moves the schema to exactly the given target version. Migrations
above the target that are applied are rolled back (descending); pending
migrations at or below the target are applied (ascending). A target of 0
rolls everything back.

methods.name: Migrations
methods.doc: Migrations returns a copy of the registered migrations in ascending order.

methods.name: Redo
methods.doc:
Redo rolls back the most recently applied migration and re-applies it. It is
a no-op when nothing has been applied.

methods.name: Register
methods.doc:
Register adds migrations, keeping the internal set sorted by Version and
rejecting duplicate versions or malformed migrations.

methods.name: Rollback
methods.signature: func (mg *Migrator) Rollback(ctx context.Context, steps int) error
methods.doc:
Rollback is a convenience alias for rolling back the given number of steps.
Rollback(ctx, 1) reverses only the most recent migration.

methods.name: Status
methods.doc:
Status returns the state of every registered migration, plus any applied
version that has no registered migration (reported with Name "(unknown)"),
sorted ascending by version.

methods.name: Up
methods.signature: func (mg *Migrator) Up(ctx context.Context, n int) error
methods.doc:
Up applies the next n pending migrations in ascending order. If n <= 0 all
pending migrations are applied (equivalent to [Migrator.Migrate]).

methods.name: Validate
methods.doc: Validate reports whether the migrator is safely configured.

methods.name: Version
methods.doc:
Version returns the highest applied migration version recorded in the
bookkeeping table, or 0 when nothing has been applied. It is useful for
stamping a [SchemaDump] with the schema's current version.

types.name: Option
types.doc: Option configures a [Migrator].

funcs.name: WithTable
funcs.doc: WithTable overrides the bookkeeping table name (default [DefaultTable]).

types.name: ReferenceOption
types.doc: ReferenceOption modifies a [Table.References] column.

funcs.name: Polymorphic
funcs.doc:
Polymorphic marks the reference polymorphic, mirroring ActiveRecord's
polymorphic: true. A companion "<name>_type" string column is emitted
alongside the "<name>_id" column and no foreign key is generated. When
combined with [ReferenceIndex] on [Schema.AddReference] the index covers the
pair ("<name>_type", "<name>_id"), matching Rails' composite polymorphic
index.

funcs.name: ReferenceIndex
funcs.doc:
ReferenceIndex adds an index on the reference column. It applies to
[Schema.AddReference]; inline table references ignore it.

funcs.name: ReferenceNotNull
funcs.doc: ReferenceNotNull marks the reference column NOT NULL.

funcs.name: ReferenceTable
funcs.doc: ReferenceTable overrides the referenced table name (default: pluralised).

funcs.name: WithForeignKey
funcs.doc: WithForeignKey emits an inline FOREIGN KEY constraint for the reference.

types.name: ReferentialAction
types.doc: ReferentialAction is an ON DELETE / ON UPDATE referential action.

types.name: Schema
types.doc:
Schema is a dialect-aware builder for schema DSL statements. Every method
returns a SQL string (or ";\n"-joined statements) rendered for the bound
[Dialect]. The package-level helpers ([CreateTable], [AddColumn], ...) are
thin wrappers around a Schema bound to [ANSI].

funcs.name: NewSchema
funcs.doc:
NewSchema returns a [Schema] that renders statements for d. A nil dialect
defaults to [ANSI].

methods.name: AddCheckConstraint
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) AddCheckConstraint(table, expression string, opts ...ConstraintOption) string
methods.doc:
AddCheckConstraint builds an ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... CHECK (expr)
statement. The expression is emitted verbatim. When [ConstraintName] is not
supplied, a deterministic name ("chk_<table>_<sanitized-expr>") is generated
so [Schema.RemoveCheckConstraint] with the same expression targets it.

methods.name: AddColumn
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) AddColumn(table, name, sqlType string, opts ...ColumnOption) string
methods.doc:
AddColumn builds an ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN statement using an explicit SQL
type spelling.

methods.name: AddEnumValue
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) AddEnumValue(enumName, value string, opts ...EnumValueOption) string
methods.doc:
AddEnumValue builds an ALTER TYPE ... ADD VALUE statement adding value to a
PostgreSQL enumerated type. By default the value is appended; use
[BeforeValue] or [AfterValue] to position it. If both are given, [BeforeValue]
takes precedence.

methods.name: AddForeignKey
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) AddForeignKey(fromTable, toTable string, opts ...ForeignKeyOption) string
methods.doc:
AddForeignKey builds an ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... FOREIGN KEY
statement linking fromTable to toTable. By default the referencing column is
"<singular-toTable>_id" and the referenced column is "id"; override with
[FKColumn] and [FKPrimaryKey]. Referential actions are added with [OnDelete]
and [OnUpdate].

methods.name: AddIndex
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) AddIndex(table string, columns []string, opts ...IndexOption) string
methods.doc:
AddIndex builds a CREATE INDEX statement over the given columns. Columns that
are bare identifiers are quoted for the dialect; anything else (such as a
functional expression "lower(email)") is emitted verbatim, enabling
expression indexes. When no name is supplied the ActiveRecord-style
"index_<table>_on_<cols>" convention is used, joining multiple columns with
"_and_" exactly as Rails' index_name does (e.g. columns foo and bar on table
testings yield "index_testings_on_foo_and_bar").

Options compose: [UniqueIndex] for uniqueness, [Using] to pick an index
method, and [Where] for a partial index.

methods.name: AddReference
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) AddReference(table, name string, opts ...ReferenceOption) string
methods.doc:
AddReference builds statement(s) adding a "<name>_id" foreign-key column to an
existing table, mirroring ActiveRecord's add_reference. By default it only
adds the column; pass [WithForeignKey] to also emit an ADD CONSTRAINT
foreign key, and [ReferenceIndex] to add an index on the new column.

With [Polymorphic] a companion "<name>_type" VARCHAR column is added ahead of
"<name>_id", no foreign key is emitted, and a requested index covers the pair
("<name>_type", "<name>_id") — matching ActiveRecord's composite polymorphic
index.

methods.name: AddTimestamps
methods.doc:
AddTimestamps builds a statement adding created_at and updated_at NOT NULL
timestamp columns to an existing table.

methods.name: AddUniqueConstraint
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) AddUniqueConstraint(table string, columns []string, opts ...ConstraintOption) string
methods.doc:
AddUniqueConstraint builds an ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... UNIQUE (cols)
statement. Unlike a unique index, a unique constraint can be referenced by a
foreign key and marked [Deferrable]. When [ConstraintName] is not supplied a
deterministic name ("uniq_<table>_<cols>") is generated.

methods.name: ChangeColumn
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) ChangeColumn(table, name, sqlType string, opts ...ColumnOption) string
methods.doc:
ChangeColumn builds a statement that changes the type of an existing column.
The exact syntax depends on the dialect: MySQL emits MODIFY COLUMN, while
PostgreSQL, SQLite, and ANSI emit ALTER COLUMN ... TYPE. The type is given as
an explicit SQL spelling.

methods.name: ChangeColumnDefault
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) ChangeColumnDefault(table, column string, value any) string
methods.doc:
ChangeColumnDefault builds an ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN ... SET DEFAULT
statement. The value is rendered with the same rules as the [Default] column
option (strings quoted, booleans as TRUE/FALSE). Use
[Schema.ChangeColumnDefaultRaw] for an expression such as CURRENT_TIMESTAMP.

methods.name: ChangeColumnDefaultRaw
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) ChangeColumnDefaultRaw(table, column, expr string) string
methods.doc:
ChangeColumnDefaultRaw builds an ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN ... SET DEFAULT
statement whose default is the given SQL expression, emitted verbatim.

methods.name: ChangeColumnNull
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) ChangeColumnNull(table, column string, null bool) string
methods.doc:
ChangeColumnNull builds a statement toggling a column's NOT NULL constraint,
mirroring ActiveRecord's change_column_null. Passing null=false adds NOT NULL;
null=true drops it. The standard "ALTER COLUMN ... SET/DROP NOT NULL" spelling
is emitted (supported by PostgreSQL, SQLite, and ANSI); MySQL requires a full
column redefinition, so use [Schema.ChangeColumn] there.

methods.name: ChangeTable
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) ChangeTable(name string, build func(t *AlterTable)) string
methods.doc:
ChangeTable builds a bulk table alteration. The build callback receives an
[AlterTable]; every operation it records is emitted in order as ";\n"-joined
statements.

methods.name: CreateEnum
methods.doc:
CreateEnum builds a CREATE TYPE ... AS ENUM statement declaring a PostgreSQL
enumerated type. The values are emitted as quoted string literals. Reference
the type from a column with [Table.EnumType].

methods.name: CreateJoinTable
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) CreateJoinTable(table1, table2 string, build func(t *Table), opts ...TableOption) string
methods.doc:
CreateJoinTable builds a CREATE TABLE statement for a many-to-many join table
linking table1 and table2. The table has no surrogate id primary key; it holds
two NOT NULL BIGINT foreign-key columns named "<singular>_id" for each side.
The optional build callback adds further columns (for example a Timestamps or
a payload column). The generated table name follows [JoinTableName].

methods.name: CreateSequence
methods.doc:
CreateSequence builds a CREATE SEQUENCE statement. Use [SequenceStart] and
[SequenceIncrement] to set the initial value and step; omitted clauses fall
back to the database defaults.

methods.name: CreateTable
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) CreateTable(name string, build func(t *Table), opts ...TableOption) string
methods.doc:
CreateTable builds a CREATE TABLE statement. Unless [WithoutID] is supplied it
prepends an auto-incrementing primary key column appropriate to the dialect,
matching the ActiveRecord convention.

methods.name: CreateView
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) CreateView(name, query string, opts ...ViewOption) string
methods.doc:
CreateView builds a CREATE VIEW statement whose body is the given SELECT
query, emitted verbatim. Combine with [OrReplace] to replace an existing view
or [Materialized] for a PostgreSQL materialized view.

methods.name: Dialect
methods.doc: Dialect returns the dialect the schema renders for.

methods.name: DisableExtension
methods.doc:
DisableExtension builds a DROP EXTENSION IF EXISTS statement, unloading a
PostgreSQL extension previously enabled with [Schema.EnableExtension].

methods.name: DropColumn
methods.doc: DropColumn builds an ALTER TABLE ... DROP COLUMN statement.

methods.name: DropColumnDefault
methods.doc:
DropColumnDefault builds an ALTER TABLE ... ALTER COLUMN ... DROP DEFAULT
statement, removing any default previously set on the column.

methods.name: DropEnum
methods.doc:
DropEnum builds a DROP TYPE statement removing an enumerated type created with
[Schema.CreateEnum].

methods.name: DropIndex
methods.doc: DropIndex builds a DROP INDEX statement.

methods.name: DropJoinTable
methods.doc:
DropJoinTable builds a DROP TABLE statement for the join table linking table1
and table2 (see [JoinTableName]).

methods.name: DropSequence
methods.doc:
DropSequence builds a DROP SEQUENCE statement removing a sequence created with
[Schema.CreateSequence].

methods.name: DropTable
methods.doc: DropTable builds a DROP TABLE statement.

methods.name: DropTableIfExists
methods.doc: DropTableIfExists builds a DROP TABLE IF EXISTS statement.

methods.name: DropView
methods.doc:
DropView builds a DROP VIEW statement. Pass [Materialized] to drop a
materialized view and [ViewIfExists] to add IF EXISTS.

methods.name: EnableExtension
methods.doc:
EnableExtension builds a CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS statement, loading a
PostgreSQL extension such as "pgcrypto" or "uuid-ossp". The extension name is
double-quoted so hyphenated names are handled correctly.

methods.name: RefreshMaterializedView
methods.doc:
RefreshMaterializedView builds a REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW statement
(PostgreSQL), recomputing a materialized view's stored result set.

methods.name: RemoveCheckConstraint
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) RemoveCheckConstraint(table, expression string, opts ...ConstraintOption) string
methods.doc:
RemoveCheckConstraint builds an ALTER TABLE ... DROP CONSTRAINT statement
removing a CHECK constraint. The same table and expression (or [ConstraintName])
that created the constraint reproduce its name here.

methods.name: RemoveForeignKey
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) RemoveForeignKey(fromTable, toTable string, opts ...ForeignKeyOption) string
methods.doc:
RemoveForeignKey builds an ALTER TABLE ... DROP CONSTRAINT statement removing
the foreign key from fromTable to toTable. The same options that named the
constraint in [Schema.AddForeignKey] reproduce the name here; pass [FKName]
to target an explicitly named constraint.

methods.name: RemoveReference
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) RemoveReference(table, name string, opts ...ReferenceOption) string
methods.doc:
RemoveReference builds statement(s) dropping a reference column previously
added with [Schema.AddReference]. A [Polymorphic] reference also drops the
companion "<name>_type" column.

methods.name: RemoveTimestamps
methods.doc:
RemoveTimestamps builds a statement dropping the created_at and updated_at
columns from a table.

methods.name: RemoveUniqueConstraint
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) RemoveUniqueConstraint(table string, columns []string, opts ...ConstraintOption) string
methods.doc:
RemoveUniqueConstraint builds an ALTER TABLE ... DROP CONSTRAINT statement
removing a unique constraint added with [Schema.AddUniqueConstraint]. The same
columns (or [ConstraintName]) reproduce the constraint name.

methods.name: RenameColumn
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) RenameColumn(table, from, to string) string
methods.doc: RenameColumn builds an ALTER TABLE ... RENAME COLUMN ... TO ... statement.

methods.name: RenameIndex
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) RenameIndex(table, from, to string) string
methods.doc:
RenameIndex builds a statement renaming an index, mirroring ActiveRecord's
rename_index. The syntax is dialect-specific: MySQL uses
"ALTER TABLE <table> RENAME INDEX <from> TO <to>", while PostgreSQL, SQLite,
and ANSI use "ALTER INDEX <from> RENAME TO <to>" (the table argument is
unused there). SQLite has no native rename; drop and recreate instead.

methods.name: RenameTable
methods.doc: RenameTable builds an ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO statement.

methods.name: SetColumnComment
methods.signature: func (s *Schema) SetColumnComment(table, column, comment string) string
methods.doc:
SetColumnComment builds a COMMENT ON COLUMN statement attaching a comment to a
column. The comment is emitted as a quoted string literal.

methods.name: SetTableComment
methods.doc:
SetTableComment builds a COMMENT ON TABLE statement attaching a comment to a
table, mirroring ActiveRecord's table comment option. The comment is emitted
as a quoted string literal.

methods.name: TruncateTable
methods.doc:
TruncateTable builds a TRUNCATE TABLE statement emptying one or more tables.
At least one table name must be given; multiple names are comma-separated.

types.name: SchemaDump
types.doc:
SchemaDump accumulates DDL statements into a single, reconstructable schema
script stamped with a schema version, analogous to ActiveRecord's schema.rb.
Running the emitted script against an empty database recreates the schema.

A dump is dialect-aware: its builder methods render statements for the bound
[Dialect]. Statements are emitted in the order they are recorded.

funcs.name: NewSchemaDump
funcs.doc:
NewSchemaDump returns a [SchemaDump] that renders statements for d (nil means
[ANSI]) and records the given schema version in the header.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add appends one or more already-rendered statements verbatim.

methods.name: AddCheckConstraint
methods.doc: AddCheckConstraint records an ADD CONSTRAINT ... CHECK statement.

methods.name: AddForeignKey
methods.doc: AddForeignKey records an ADD CONSTRAINT foreign key statement.

methods.name: AddIndex
methods.doc: AddIndex records a CREATE INDEX statement.

methods.name: AddUniqueConstraint
methods.doc: AddUniqueConstraint records an ADD CONSTRAINT ... UNIQUE statement.

methods.name: CreateEnum
methods.doc: CreateEnum records a CREATE TYPE ... AS ENUM statement.

methods.name: CreateJoinTable
methods.signature: func (s *SchemaDump) CreateJoinTable(table1, table2 string, build func(t *Table), opts ...TableOption) *SchemaDump
methods.doc: CreateJoinTable records a join-table CREATE TABLE statement.

methods.name: CreateTable
methods.signature: func (s *SchemaDump) CreateTable(name string, build func(t *Table), opts ...TableOption) *SchemaDump
methods.doc: CreateTable records a CREATE TABLE statement.

methods.name: CreateView
methods.doc: CreateView records a CREATE VIEW statement.

methods.name: EnableExtension
methods.doc: EnableExtension records a CREATE EXTENSION statement.

methods.name: SetVersion
methods.doc:
SetVersion updates the stamped schema version and returns the dump for
chaining.

methods.name: Statements
methods.doc: Statements returns the recorded statements in order.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String renders the full dump: a header comment carrying the dialect and
schema version, followed by every recorded statement terminated by ";". The
output is valid SQL that reconstructs the schema.

methods.name: Version
methods.doc: Version returns the schema version stamped on the dump.

types.name: SeedOption
types.doc: SeedOption configures a [Seeder].

funcs.name: WithSeedTable
funcs.doc:
WithSeedTable overrides the seed bookkeeping table name (default
[DefaultSeedTable]).

types.name: Seeder
types.doc:
Seeder loads data into the database idempotently. Each seed is identified by a
unique name; once a seed has run successfully its name is recorded, and
subsequent runs skip it. This makes seed loading safe to invoke on every boot,
mirroring the intent of Rails' db/seeds with added tracking.

A Seeder is not safe for concurrent use against the same database; run seeds
from a single goroutine.

funcs.name: NewSeeder
funcs.doc: NewSeeder creates a Seeder over db.

methods.name: Applied
methods.doc: Applied returns the set of seed names that have already run.

methods.name: EnsureTable
methods.doc: EnsureTable creates the seed bookkeeping table if it does not already exist.

methods.name: Run
methods.signature: func (s *Seeder) Run(ctx context.Context, name string, fn func(ctx context.Context, tx *sql.Tx) error) error
methods.doc:
Run executes the named seed inside its own transaction and records it, but
only if it has not already run. If the seed has run before, Run is a no-op and
returns nil. The fn callback receives the transaction and must not commit or
roll it back; the Seeder owns the transaction lifecycle. A failing fn rolls
the transaction back and the seed is not recorded.

methods.name: RunSQL
methods.signature: func (s *Seeder) RunSQL(ctx context.Context, name string, statements ...string) error
methods.doc:
RunSQL is a convenience wrapper around [Seeder.Run] that executes one or more
raw SQL statements as the seed body.

types.name: SequenceOption
types.doc: SequenceOption modifies a [Schema.CreateSequence] statement.

funcs.name: SequenceIncrement
funcs.doc: SequenceIncrement sets the sequence's step (INCREMENT BY n).

funcs.name: SequenceStart
funcs.doc: SequenceStart sets the sequence's initial value (START WITH n).

types.name: Table
types.doc: Table accumulates column definitions inside a [Schema.CreateTable] block.

methods.name: BigInteger
methods.doc: BigInteger adds a 64-bit integer column.

methods.name: Binary
methods.doc: Binary adds a binary large object column.

methods.name: Boolean
methods.doc: Boolean adds a boolean column.

methods.name: Column
methods.doc:
Column adds a column with an explicit SQL type spelling. For DSL type helpers
use the typed [Table] methods instead.

methods.name: Date
methods.doc: Date adds a calendar-date column.

methods.name: Decimal
methods.signature: func (t *Table) Decimal(name string, precision, scale int, opts ...ColumnOption)
methods.doc:
Decimal adds a fixed-point DECIMAL(precision, scale) column. A precision of
zero omits the size specifier entirely.

methods.name: Enum
methods.doc:
Enum adds an enumerated string column restricted to values. On PostgreSQL the
column references a named type (see [Table.EnumType]); MySQL emits an inline
ENUM; other dialects fall back to VARCHAR.

methods.name: EnumType
methods.signature: func (t *Table) EnumType(name, typeName string, values []string, opts ...ColumnOption)
methods.doc:
EnumType adds an enumerated string column that references a pre-declared
PostgreSQL type named typeName.

methods.name: Float
methods.doc: Float adds a double-precision floating point column.

methods.name: Integer
methods.doc: Integer adds a 32-bit integer column.

methods.name: JSON
methods.doc: JSON adds a textual JSON column.

methods.name: JSONB
methods.doc:
JSONB adds a binary JSON column (PostgreSQL jsonb; other dialects fall back to
their JSON type).

methods.name: References
methods.doc:
References adds a "<name>_id" foreign-key column. By default it only adds the
column; pass [WithForeignKey] to also emit an inline REFERENCES constraint to
the pluralised table's id column.

When [Polymorphic] is set the reference is polymorphic: a companion
"<name>_type" VARCHAR column is added ahead of the "<name>_id" column,
mirroring ActiveRecord's t.references :taggable, polymorphic: true. A
polymorphic reference never emits a foreign key, since it may point at more
than one table.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String adds a VARCHAR column (VARCHAR(255) unless [Limit] is given).

methods.name: Text
methods.doc: Text adds a TEXT column.

methods.name: Time
methods.doc: Time adds a time-of-day column.

methods.name: Timestamp
methods.doc:
Timestamp adds a single date-and-time column. Combine with [Precision] and
[WithTimezone] for fractional-second precision and time-zone awareness.

methods.name: Timestamps
methods.doc:
Timestamps adds the conventional created_at / updated_at NOT NULL timestamp
columns.

methods.name: UUID
methods.doc: UUID adds a universally-unique-identifier column.

types.name: TableOption
types.doc: TableOption modifies a CreateTable statement.

funcs.name: IfNotExists
funcs.doc: IfNotExists adds IF NOT EXISTS to the CREATE TABLE statement.

funcs.name: WithoutID
funcs.doc: WithoutID suppresses the automatic identity primary key column.

types.name: ViewOption
types.doc:
ViewOption modifies a view statement produced by [Schema.CreateView] and
[Schema.DropView].

funcs.name: Materialized
funcs.doc:
Materialized renders a MATERIALIZED VIEW (PostgreSQL), whose result set is
stored on disk and refreshed with [Schema.RefreshMaterializedView].

funcs.name: OrReplace
funcs.doc:
OrReplace renders CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW, replacing an existing view in place.
It is ignored for materialized views, which cannot be replaced this way.

funcs.name: ViewIfExists
funcs.doc: ViewIfExists adds IF EXISTS to a [Schema.DropView] statement.

funcs.name: AddCheckConstraint
funcs.signature: func AddCheckConstraint(table, expression string, opts ...ConstraintOption) string
funcs.doc: AddCheckConstraint builds a CHECK constraint using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: AddColumn
funcs.signature: func AddColumn(table, name, sqlType string, opts ...ColumnOption) string
funcs.doc:
AddColumn builds an ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN statement using the [ANSI]
dialect and an explicit SQL type spelling.

funcs.name: AddForeignKey
funcs.signature: func AddForeignKey(fromTable, toTable string, opts ...ForeignKeyOption) string
funcs.doc: AddForeignKey builds a foreign-key constraint using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: AddIndex
funcs.signature: func AddIndex(table string, columns []string, opts ...IndexOption) string
funcs.doc: AddIndex builds a CREATE INDEX statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: AddReference
funcs.signature: func AddReference(table, name string, opts ...ReferenceOption) string
funcs.doc: AddReference builds a reference-column addition using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: AddTimestamps
funcs.doc: AddTimestamps builds created_at/updated_at additions using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: AddUniqueConstraint
funcs.signature: func AddUniqueConstraint(table string, columns []string, opts ...ConstraintOption) string
funcs.doc: AddUniqueConstraint builds a UNIQUE constraint using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: ChangeColumn
funcs.signature: func ChangeColumn(table, name, sqlType string, opts ...ColumnOption) string
funcs.doc: ChangeColumn builds a column type change using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: ChangeColumnDefault
funcs.signature: func ChangeColumnDefault(table, column string, value any) string
funcs.doc: ChangeColumnDefault builds a SET DEFAULT statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: ChangeColumnDefaultRaw
funcs.signature: func ChangeColumnDefaultRaw(table, column, expr string) string
funcs.doc:
ChangeColumnDefaultRaw builds a raw-expression SET DEFAULT statement using the
[ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: ChangeColumnNull
funcs.signature: func ChangeColumnNull(table, column string, null bool) string
funcs.doc: ChangeColumnNull builds a SET/DROP NOT NULL statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: ChangeTable
funcs.signature: func ChangeTable(name string, build func(t *AlterTable)) string
funcs.doc: ChangeTable builds a bulk table alteration using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: CreateEnum
funcs.doc: CreateEnum builds a CREATE TYPE ... AS ENUM statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: CreateJoinTable
funcs.signature: func CreateJoinTable(table1, table2 string, build func(t *Table), opts ...TableOption) string
funcs.doc: CreateJoinTable builds a join-table creation using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: CreateSequence
funcs.doc: CreateSequence builds a CREATE SEQUENCE statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: CreateTable
funcs.signature: func CreateTable(name string, build func(t *Table), opts ...TableOption) string
funcs.doc:
CreateTable builds a CREATE TABLE statement using the [ANSI] dialect. See
[Schema.CreateTable] for the dialect-aware form.

funcs.name: CreateView
funcs.signature: func CreateView(name, query string, opts ...ViewOption) string
funcs.doc: CreateView builds a CREATE VIEW statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: DisableExtension
funcs.doc: DisableExtension builds a DROP EXTENSION statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: DropColumn
funcs.doc:
DropColumn builds an ALTER TABLE ... DROP COLUMN statement using the [ANSI]
dialect.

funcs.name: DropColumnDefault
funcs.doc: DropColumnDefault builds a DROP DEFAULT statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: DropEnum
funcs.doc: DropEnum builds a DROP TYPE statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: DropIndex
funcs.doc: DropIndex builds a DROP INDEX statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: DropJoinTable
funcs.doc: DropJoinTable builds a join-table drop using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: DropSequence
funcs.doc: DropSequence builds a DROP SEQUENCE statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: DropTable
funcs.doc: DropTable builds a DROP TABLE statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: DropTableIfExists
funcs.doc:
DropTableIfExists builds a DROP TABLE IF EXISTS statement using the [ANSI]
dialect.

funcs.name: DropView
funcs.doc: DropView builds a DROP VIEW statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: EnableExtension
funcs.doc: EnableExtension builds a CREATE EXTENSION statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: Execute
funcs.signature: func Execute(ctx context.Context, tx *sql.Tx, query string, args ...any) error
funcs.doc:
Execute runs a single raw SQL statement inside tx. It is a small convenience
for Go migrations and seeds that want to run one statement without threading
the result value.

funcs.name: ExecuteAll
funcs.signature: func ExecuteAll(ctx context.Context, tx *sql.Tx, statements ...string) error
funcs.doc:
ExecuteAll runs several raw SQL statements inside tx in order, stopping at the
first error.

funcs.name: JoinTableName
funcs.doc:
JoinTableName returns the conventional join-table name for two tables: the two
names sorted lexicographically and joined with an underscore, matching
ActiveRecord's default (e.g. JoinTableName("parts", "assemblies") ==
"assemblies_parts").

funcs.name: RemoveCheckConstraint
funcs.signature: func RemoveCheckConstraint(table, expression string, opts ...ConstraintOption) string
funcs.doc: RemoveCheckConstraint builds a CHECK constraint removal using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: RemoveForeignKey
funcs.signature: func RemoveForeignKey(fromTable, toTable string, opts ...ForeignKeyOption) string
funcs.doc: RemoveForeignKey builds a foreign-key removal using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: RemoveReference
funcs.signature: func RemoveReference(table, name string, opts ...ReferenceOption) string
funcs.doc: RemoveReference builds a reference-column removal using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: RemoveTimestamps
funcs.doc:
RemoveTimestamps builds created_at/updated_at removals using the [ANSI]
dialect.

funcs.name: RemoveUniqueConstraint
funcs.signature: func RemoveUniqueConstraint(table string, columns []string, opts ...ConstraintOption) string
funcs.doc: RemoveUniqueConstraint builds a UNIQUE constraint removal using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: RenameColumn
funcs.signature: func RenameColumn(table, from, to string) string
funcs.doc:
RenameColumn builds an ALTER TABLE ... RENAME COLUMN statement using the
[ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: RenameIndex
funcs.signature: func RenameIndex(table, from, to string) string
funcs.doc: RenameIndex builds an index-rename statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: RenameTable
funcs.doc:
RenameTable builds an ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO statement using the [ANSI]
dialect.

funcs.name: SetColumnComment
funcs.signature: func SetColumnComment(table, column, comment string) string
funcs.doc: SetColumnComment builds a COMMENT ON COLUMN statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: SetTableComment
funcs.doc: SetTableComment builds a COMMENT ON TABLE statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

funcs.name: TruncateTable
funcs.doc: TruncateTable builds a TRUNCATE TABLE statement using the [ANSI] dialect.

examples.output:
after migrate:
  20240101 create_users applied
  20240102 add_users_name applied
after rollback 1:
  20240101 create_users applied
  20240102 add_users_name pending
examples.doc:
Example demonstrates a full migrate / status / rollback cycle against the
in-memory test driver. Real programs would open a driver such as "sqlite" or
"mysql" instead of "memdb".
